response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, senior full-stack engineer with a strong craftsmanship background, but the profile is fundamentally mismatched with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel analysis and modification, and embedded LAN-switch security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking) — none of which appear on the resume. His C/C++ exposure is brief and dated (a seven-month stint in 2014-2015), and his career centers on web platforms in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than embedded firmware or network security.
strengths: Long, senior track record across web, mobile, and backend systems with strong testing and TDD discipline
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Comfortable on Linux with DevOps, CI/CD, and open-source tooling contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed; the role requires native-level Japanese
No Linux kernel, embedded firmware, or C-for-UNIX depth; C/C++ experience is minimal and dated
No L2/L3 networking, switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)

key_factors.factor: No embedded C / Linux kernel / network-security experience
Strong general engineering seniority and Linux familiarity
